findings.title: 'bob' can DELETE id=7 but GET returns 403
findings.description: Access control is inconsistent across HTTP verbs. The user cannot read the resource (GET → 403) but the DELETE succeeded with status 200. This is a classic authorization seam — the write path likely skips the ownership check.

findings.title: Unauthenticated access to GET public health check
findings.description: The endpoint returned a 200 with 512 bytes of content to an unauthenticated request. Resources expecting authentication should return 401/403.

findings.title: IDOR: user 'bob' can GET resource owned by 'alice'
findings.description: Both users received functionally identical responses (hash match or <10% length drift). This indicates 'bob' has unauthorized GET access to a resource that appears to belong to 'alice'.
